nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Berges
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alvaro, Jose, Carlos, Cesar, Florina, Gustavo, Luis, Margarita, Mariana, Mercedes, Ramon, Xavier.
German (Lower Rhine): topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, from a shortened form of   Berghaus  . Compare   Berkes  .
French (Bergès): Gascon variant of Vergès (see Verges ).

variants.name: Berghaus
meanings: German: topographic name denoting a house on a hill, a compound of Berg ‘mountain, hill’ + Haus ‘house’. Compare Berges .

variants.name: Berkes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto.
North German: topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, shortened from Berg-hūs (Berg ‘mountain, hill’ + Low German hūs ‘house’). It is a variant of Berkus or Berges .
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal names Bernát or Bertalan, Hungarian forms of Bernard and Bartholomew , respectively.

nameMeanings.name: Prestwold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Shropshire and Lincolnshire): habitational name from any of several places called from Middle English prest, pri(e)st ‘priest’ + wode ‘wood’ (Old English prēost, wudu).

variants.name: Presswood
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Prestwood  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold was born in Leicestershire, England as the son of Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds. He had at least 1 son. He died in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold est né à Leicestershire, Angleterre, fils de Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold wurde in Leicestershire, England, als Sohn von Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold è nato il nel Leicestershire, England come figlio di Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold(은)는 영국 레이스스터실르에서 Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Anchetil De Berges De Prestwold родился в местности лисестиршир, Англия. Его отца звали Lord Hugh de Berges of Burton on the Wolds. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
